creator: evd_sas@mail.ru
text:
In netstats output field "Netif" to small:

# netstat -nr | more
Routing tables

Internet:
Destination        Gateway            Flags    Refs      Use  Netif Expire
default            89.221.63.225      UG1         0 44617065 vlan10
3                  89.221.63.225      UG1         0      108 vlan10
4/9                89.221.63.225      UG1         0    10390 vlan10 =>
4                  89.221.63.225      UG1         0        0 vlan10
4.128/9            89.221.63.225      UG1         0        4 vlan10
6.10/15            89.221.63.225      UG1         0        0 vlan10
6.14/15            89.221.63.225      UG1         0        0 vlan10


but I haven't had vlan10, I have
# ifconfig | grep vlan
vlan102: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> mtu 1500
        vlan: 102 parent interface: em1
vlan101: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> mtu 1500
        vlan: 101 parent interface: em1
vlan103: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> mtu 1500
        vlan: 103 parent interface: em1
vlan104: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> mtu 1500
        vlan: 104 parent interface: em1
vlan120: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> mtu 1500
        vlan: 120 parent interface: em1
vlan121: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> mtu 1500
        vlan: 121 parent interface: em1
vlan105: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> mtu 1500
        vlan: 105 parent interface: em1

It's not a problem, it's not good.
